channel.title: POLITICO Influence
channel.description: Delivered daily, Influence gives you a comprehensive rundown and analysis of all lobby hires and news on K Street.

item.title: Lobbying fight heats up ahead of deepfakes markup
item.description: Tech companies, labor groups and free speech organizations are making their competing cases to lawmakers on the NO FAKES Act as Congress considers several pieces of legislation focused on artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: The latest crypto midterms test
item.description: President Donald Trump is looming large in several races across the South tonight. And one race in particular will test another key midterms player: the crypto industry.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Vogel Group hires Blanche chief
item.description: Bipartisan lobbying firm Vogel Group has scooped up Brian Nieves, former chief of staff to acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Research world rallies against new grant rules
item.description: Nonprofits, research institutions and scientists are going to bat with the Office of Management and Budget over a proposed rule that would overhaul the federal government’s process for awarding research grants.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Thorn Run taps a Trump alum
item.description: Former White House Policy Adviser Kamran Daravi is teaming up with bipartisan lobbying firm Thorn Run Partners to help its clients navigate Washington under the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: OpenAI findings boost GOP claims of foreign influence
item.description: OpenAI published new research this afternoon claiming China was likely behind two online influence operations intended to sway U.S. perceptions of artificial intelligence technology and reshape the debate in Washington around the infrastructure needed to support it.
item.dc:creator: By Dana Nickel, Yasmin Khorram and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Dana Nickel, Yasmin Khorram and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Lobbying fight heats up over Israel provision
item.description: An obscure provision in the National Defense Authorization Act is sparking a fierce lobbying battle over the future of U.S.-Israeli military cooperation — and reigniting tensions over U.S. support for Israel.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Meet the jet-setting influence broker on Venezuela policy
item.description: Former Illinois Republican lawmaker Aaron Schock has had a rough decade since resigning from Congress in 2015 amid an ethics investigation into alleged spending abuses. But when President Donald Trump returned to power, he saw an opportunity to return to the political scene — and potentially strike gold in Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: By Adam Wren, Eric Bazail-Eimil, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Adam Wren, Eric Bazail-Eimil, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Behind Polymarket’s paid influencer campaign
item.description: Social media is swamped with posts promoting prediction market platforms as money-making opportunities and helpful forecasting tools. But many of the creators promoting one of the industry’s top players, Polymarket, have also been receiving payments from the company’s chief marketing officer without disclosing them.
item.dc:creator: By Jason Beeferman, Maya Kaufman, Jessie Blaeser, Declan Harty and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jason Beeferman, Maya Kaufman, Jessie Blaeser, Declan Harty and Jacob Wendler

item.title: An ‘intel operation’ against liberal nonprofits
item.description: The Trump administration has been unrelenting in its efforts to target liberal nonprofits, and one group has been instrumental in supplying the GOP with the research to do so: the Capital Research Center.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Robotics lobby presses for NDAA wins
item.description: The robotics lobby is bracing for the upcoming markups of the annual National Defense Authorization Act as the industry looks for opportunities to score policy wins in the robotics race with China.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: It’s an AI-filled day (and night)
item.description: President Donald Trump quietly signed an executive order on artificial intelligence Tuesday. One tech lobbyist says he anticipates major AI labs will be “quietly relieved” it didn’t go further.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Labor's big data center moment
item.description: With tech companies, state lawmakers and AI skeptics battling over the future of data centers, labor unions are joining the fight to let Silicon Valley keep building.
item.dc:creator: By Jason Plautz, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jason Plautz, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: New ad campaign hits Dems on crypto bill
item.description: A new ad campaign from centrist-Democratic group New Democracy is taking aim at the CLARITY Act, a key priority for Congress and the White House that the group claims doesn’t reflect what Americans want from their representatives.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Trump admin hit by more self-dealing allegations
item.description: A flurry of headlines in the last day have added fuel to Democrats’ arguments that President Donald Trump and his allies are improperly profiting from his administration.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Family of fed kidnapped in Iran enlists K St help
item.description: The family of a former federal agent who disappeared during a CIA mission in Iran has hired Ballard Partners and BGR Group to help obtain nearly $1.5 billion in damages from the Iranian government, disclosures show.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: The latest Chinese espionage charge
item.description: Thomas Pauken II is facing a criminal charge of acting as an agent for the Chinese government.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler, Josh Gerstein and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler, Josh Gerstein and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Inside the White House’s AI reversal
item.description: The future of a keenly awaited executive order on artificial intelligence is in jeopardy after Thursday’s abrupt reversal, leaving the tech industry bracing once again for a possible new framework for government oversight.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Lippman
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler and Daniel Lippman

item.title: DoorDash steps up its lobbying game
item.description: DoorDash has unmistakably become more visible on the federal stage in recent years, a development that its head of public policy Max Rettig attributes to the 13-year-old company’s expansion beyond restaurant delivery into reservations and retail delivery.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Inside OpenAI’s state-by-state lobbying strategy
item.description: OpenAI has a plan to deal with the wave of legislation aimed at regulating artificial intelligence: influence bills in statehouses across the country, one state at a time, on the heels of successful lobbying campaigns in California and New York.
item.dc:creator: By Brendan Bordelon, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Brendan Bordelon, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: A new player in clean energy advocacy
item.description: There’s a new player in the clean energy advocacy space — spurred by the policy failures of the industry.
item.dc:creator: By Debra Kahn, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Debra Kahn, Jacob Wendler and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Prediction markets group makes first outside hire
item.description: The Coalition for Prediction Markets has hired a team of Democrats from Invariant as its first outside lobbyists amid growing congressional scrutiny over potential insider trading and intensifying regulatory disputes.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler

item.title: Hospitals’ clout in Washington is waning
item.description: Two of the most powerful lobbies in the country are turning on a third in the hopes of deflecting Washington’s wrath and securing bigger shares of the $5 trillion Americans spend on health care each year.
item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Chu and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Law firms continue fight against Trump EOs
item.description: President Donald Trump faces a tough battle to restore his executive orders targeting four major law firms following Thursday's arguments before a largely skeptical panel of three federal appellate judges.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Layoffs hit Penta Group
item.description: One of Washington’s largest public affairs consulting firms Penta Group laid off 37 staffers in the U.S. and Europe Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: The junk food lobby is winning on Capitol Hill
item.description: The “Make America Healthy Again” assault on junk food isn’t catching on in Congress, even as President Donald Trump and his supporters use the populist health movement to take shots at the deep-pocketed industry.
item.dc:creator: By Jennifer Scholtes and Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Jennifer Scholtes and Daniel Barnes

item.title: Ex-Dem fundraiser seeks a Trump pardon
item.description: Once-prolific Democratic fundraiser Hassan Nemazee has hired Mercury Public Affairs to lobby for a presidential pardon, according to a disclosure report filed Friday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Former Moroccan ambassador seeks pardon
item.description: A former Moroccan ambassador to the United Nations who was indicted in 2019 on visa fraud charges plans to seek a presidential pardon, according to a FARA registration filed with the Justice Department Thursday.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes

item.title: Farragut lands a White House hire
item.description: Richard Chalkey is leaving the White House’s Office of Legislative Affairs to join Farragut Partners as a partner, the latest staffer to ditch Pennsylvania Ave for K Street.
item.dc:creator: By Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Jacob Wendler

item.title: Poll suggests businesses should engage Trump on policy more
item.description: Speaking out against the Trump administration on policy differences may not be as risky as corporate leaders think, according to a poll released Wednesday from the Public Affairs Council and Morning Consult.
item.dc:creator: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Daniel Barnes and Jacob Wendler
